vulnerability.reference: Infrastructure operators who base resource staging decisions solely on deterministic gust forecasts without considering model error patterns.
vulnerability.generated: Operators' resource allocation decisions depend entirely on forecast accuracy, lacking independent means to detect unrealistic gust field distortions.

impact.reference: Power restoration crews positioned in wrong areas, extending blackouts and economic losses.
impact.generated: Misplaced pre-positioning of repair crews and equipment prolongs power outages and escalates economic losses from storm damage.

dose_response.reference: Double penalty loss enables distorted wind gust fields, which in extratropical storm forecasting misleads infrastructure operators, causing misplacement of repair crews and prolonged outages.
dose_response.generated: Double penalty spherical harmonic loss functions generating unrealistic high-resolution details, through infrastructure operators using wind forecasts for damage pre-positioning, trigger operators’ reliance on forecast accuracy without distortion detection, leading to misplaced repair crews that prolong outages and escalate economic losses.
